title: T-126 — WIRING: D365 modals wire into FA detail + Build/Wizard launch + SSE — slice of T-046
prompt:
# T-126 — D365 modal wiring (slice of T-046)

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md §10.6, §10.6.1
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Parent root: T-046
Depends on: T-123 (Build modal), T-124 (Wizard 1-4), T-125 (Wizard 5-8 + SSE)

## Goal
Wire D365 Build modal and 8-step Wizard modal into the FA detail page so the Build button opens D365BuildModal, the wizard launches from the Build modal (or its own entry), and SSE connects end-to-end. No console errors during the flow.

## Implementation contract
- Add Build entry point in FA detail (the appropriate D365 tab) that opens `D365BuildModal` via URL param `?modal=d365Build`.
- Add path from Build modal to Wizard launch (`?modal=d365Wizard`) and ensure both reuse the SSE route from T-125.
- Ensure RSC pre-fetches BOM preview / allergen / constants and passes as props to the modals.
- Wiring test covers: open Build modal from FA detail, transition to Wizard, SSE connection established, no console errors.

## Files in scope
- `apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[id]/_tabs/<d365-tab>.tsx [modify]` (Build button → modal trigger)
- `apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[id]/page.tsx [modify]` (modal URL param parsing if needed)
- `apps/web/app/(npd)/_modals/__tests__/d365-wiring.test.tsx [create]`

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the user is on FA detail, when they click Build, then `D365BuildModal` opens (URL contains `?modal=d365Build`).
2. Given the Build modal exposes a Launch Wizard control (or the FA exposes a wizard entry), when activated, then `D365WizardModal` opens and the SSE connection to `/(npd)/fa/d365-progress-stream` is established.
3. Given the flow completes from FA → Build modal → Wizard, when observed, then no console errors occur and modals close cleanly.
4. UI closeout includes wiring test results and any Playwright traces produced.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: `d365-wiring.test.tsx` covering FA → Build modal, Build → Wizard, SSE connection assertion.
- Run: `pnpm --filter web test d365-wiring`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not modify modal component internals beyond minimal hooks (changes belong in T-123/T-124/T-125)
- Do not bypass MFA before invoking execute phase
- Do not run Playwright parity here (T-127)
